title: asclepias glaucescens var. elata
intro: Asclepias glaucescens var. elata, also known as the Tall Green Antelopehorn Milkweed, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to the presence of cardenolides, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Asclepias glaucescens var. elata include its potential use in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. Traditionally, the plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, with the dried roots and leaves being used in herbal preparations.
